data.title: Family Research Council, Senator Graham, and state AGs to lead press conference urging FDA on abortion policies.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, at 1:00 PM ET, Family Research Council President Tony Perkins will participate in a gathering alongside U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), the attorneys general from Louisiana and Ohio, and key pro-life lawmakers from both state and national levels. The meeting is set to address important issues related to the pro-life movement and is expected to involve prominent figures in discussing strategies and initiatives.

data.title: BODYBAR Pilates is opening its first Colorado studio in Littleton in March 2026, with exclusive promotions.
data.description: A popular fitness concept is making its debut in Colorado with BODYBAR Pilates opening its first location in Littleton in early March. The high-energy, reformer-based Pilates studio is set to offer exclusive grand opening promotions. This marks the first venture of BODYBAR Pilates into the Colorado market, bringing their unique fitness experience to residents. Scheduled to open in early March, the studio is anticipated to attract fitness enthusiasts seeking a new workout regimen. This expansion signifies the growing popularity of BODYBAR Pilates, known for its dynamic workouts that combine strength training with Pilates principles. Residents of Littleton can look forward to experiencing the latest fitness trends as the studio aims to make a positive impact on the local community.

data.title: Apply by March 31 for Automotive Scholarships - Hundreds of scholarships totaling over $900,000 to be awarded by UAF.
data.description: Bethesda, Maryland - On January 13, 2026, the University of the Aftermarket Foundation (UAF) announced its plan to award a substantial number of scholarships in the automotive, heavy duty, and tire industries totaling over $900,000. This marks a record amount to be distributed to deserving students. Those interested in applying for these scholarships are encouraged to submit their applications by a specified deadline.

data.title: Livin'in seeks partners for 2026 activations focused on healthier, connected neighborhoods. ATLANTA, Jan. 13, 2026.
data.description: Livin'in, operated by ImpactMS LLC, has announced partner interest for its 2026 activations in Atlanta. The company focuses on arts-centric participation, innovative learning paths, and technology-driven experiences. These initiatives aim to provide shared credit and clear results.

data.title: KBRA summarizes CREFC January Conference Day 1; 2400+ attendees in Miami, major gathering of CRE participants.
data.description: KBRA has issued a summary of the CRE Finance Council (CREFC) January Conference 2026, which was held in Miami. The event set a new attendance record with over 2,400 participants, exceeding last year's numbers. CREFC, representing over 400 member firms, brought together professionals from various sectors of the commercial real estate (CRE) industry for discussions on key factors impacting CRE and future driving forces.

data.title: Senate bill gives XRP, SOL, DOGE BTC-like status as commodities, not securities. Escaping SEC rules, making it crucial.
data.description: The Senate Banking Committee has introduced a draft bill that proposes to categorize XRP, Solana, Dogecoin, and other cryptocurrencies as commodities rather than securities, similar to Bitcoin. The draft outlines that these tokens can be considered "non-ancillary assets" if they are the main asset of an ETF listed on a national securities exchange by January 1. This classification exempts them from certain SEC securities regulations and disclosure requirements. Altcoins such as XRP, SOL, DOGE, LTC, HBAR, and LINK are eligible for this treatment based on existing ETFs. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Texas Southern University receives Carnegie Community Engagement Classification, first HBCU in Texas to achieve this honor.
data.description: In a significant achievement, Texas Southern University in Houston has become the first Historically Black College and University (HBCU) in Texas to earn both research and community engagement designations. The university received the prestigious Carnegie Community Engagement Classification on January 13, 2026, marking a milestone for the institution. This recognition underscores Texas Southern University's commitment to academic research and community involvement, showcasing its dedication to both scholarly endeavors and serving the community.

data.title: Crypto market update: BTC +1.5% to $92K; ETH +1% to $3,130; XRP +1% to $2.06; XMR hits ATH at $680. Gold/Silver reach new highs, US drafts Crypto Market Clarity Act, Senator Warren presses SEC, Vitalik Buterin advocates decentralized stablecoins. BitGo files for $2B IPO. Tennessee orders Polymarket, Kalshi, and Crypto.com to halt sports prediction markets.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies are seeing gains, with Bitcoin (BTC) up by 1.5% to $92,000, Ethereum (ETH) rising by 1% to $3,130, Solana (SOL) increasing by 2% to $142, and XRP climbing by 1% to $2.06. Dash surged by 60%, IP by 30%, and XMR by 13%, with XMR hitting a new all-time high at $680 (currently at $640). Gold and Silver also reached new all-time highs amidst the Powell investigation. The US Senate unveiled the Crypto Market Clarity Act, which includes restrictions on stablecoin rewards. Senator Warren pushed the SEC on the inclusion of crypto in retirement accounts, citing excessive risk for retirees. Vitalik Buterin emphasized the necessity for improved decentralized stablecoins due to governance and inflation threats. World Liberty Financial introduced a crypto lending platform backed by its USD1 stablecoin, attracting around $20 million. BitGo initiated the process for a U.S. IPO aiming for a valuation of about $2 billion as custody assets surpassed $100 billion. Tennessee regulators mandated the suspension of sports prediction markets by platforms such as Polymarket, Kalshi, and Crypto.com, with a requirement to refund users, marking an escalation in a legal dispute across multiple states.

data.title: Delta Air Lines beats Q4 expectations with adjusted earnings at $1.55 per share, but guidance disappoints analysts with midpoint below consensus. Maintaining Buy rating with price target of $77.
data.description: Delta Air Lines Inc (NYSE: DAL) recently released its fourth-quarter earnings, slightly exceeding expectations. However, the company’s guidance fell short of consensus at the midpoint, according to insights from Goldman Sachs. Analyst Catherine O'Brien, from Goldman Sachs, has maintained a Buy rating for Delta Air Lines with a price target set at $77. The airline reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.55 per share, surpassing both the consensus of $1.53 per share and its own reduced guidance of approximately $1.50 per share. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: South African CPA shifts from boardroom to mission field in Christian memoir; wanted to be millionaire by 30.
data.description: In 1988, Eddie Baker, a successful author, set a goal to become a millionaire by 30. Yet, a pivotal encounter altered his course. Berlin, on Jan. 13, 2026 - Eddie's  journey began with ambitions to amass wealth, but his definition of success evolved, leading him to his true calling.

data.title: Lobos 1707 named Official Tequila Sponsor of PRO Skijor Frontier Tour, launching North America's 1st pro skijoring series.
data.description: Check out the latest PRO Skijor video featuring the collaboration with Lobos 1707, the Official Tequila Sponsor. This partnership brings Lobos 1707's Premium West brand into the spotlight alongside the launch of North America's pioneering professional skijoring series. The series, known as PRO Skijor, will kick off with its inaugural Frontier Tour. The announcement was made in New York on January 13, 2026. 1707 Lobos has secured a significant presence as the official tequila sponsor for this exciting venture.

data.title: Megan M. Reilly appointed as Director of Administration & Special Counsel at Glenn Agre Bergman & Fuentes LLP from Jan 1, 2026.
data.description: A law firm based in New York, Glenn Agre Bergman & Fuentes LLP, recently promoted Megan M. Reilly, a skilled complex commercial litigation attorney, to the position of director of administration and special counsel, starting from January 1, 2026. This role, which combines administrative responsibilities with legal counsel duties, marks a significant development in Reilly's career.

data.title: Chili's Grill and Bar renew partnership with Spire Motorsports until 2026, sponsoring No. 77 Chevy ZL1 driven by Carson Hocevar.
data.description: In a recent announcement on January 13, 2026, a popular restaurant brand and a growing race team have extended their sponsorship for the No. 77 Chevy ZL1, which is driven by Carson Hocevar. The partnership, which has captured the attention of NASCAR enthusiasts, involves promoting the brand through various engaging initiatives such as a country music video, a mechanical margarita shaker ride, and more. This collaboration signifies a significant expansion in their support for the racing team and highlights their commitment to connecting with a broader audience.

data.title: Greenberg Traurig advises Joint Lead Managers on Kuwait Finance House's $1 billion Sukuk issuance due 2031.
data.description: In Dubai, United Arab Emirates, on January 13, 2026, legal representatives from Greenberg Traurig aided the Joint Lead Managers in Kuwait Finance House's (KFH) recent Sukuk offering of US$1 billion trust certificates set to mature in 2031. The issuance was facilitated by KFH Sukuk Company as part of its larger US$4 billion financial program...

data.title: Inventor from Sparta, Wisc., creates new mobile hunting shack with strategic windows for hidden hunting.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 13, 2026, an inventor from Sparta, Wisconsin shared his inspiration for creating a unique mobile hunting shack with strategic windows to facilitate discreet and quiet hunting. This innovative invention, known as the Specially Designed Hunting Shack, aims to provide hunters with a comfortable and warm shelter while out in the wilderness. The inventor's vision for the shack incorporates elements that enhance the hunting experience, making it an ideal choice for outdoor enthusiasts.

data.title: Medical Solutions CEO Rebecca Rogers Tijerino named to Staffing 100 NA list by SIA, marking her achievement in healthcare workforce.
data.description: In Omaha, Nebraska on January 13, 2026, Medical Solutions, a top healthcare workforce partner, is excited to announce the recognition of their CEO, Rebecca Rogers Tijerino. She has been honored by Staffing as one of the notable individuals on the 2026 Staffing 100 North America list. This prestigious list acknowledges the most influential figures in the staffing industry. Tijerino’s inclusion highlights her leadership and impact in the sector.

data.title: Gas turbine market projected to reach USD 54.9 billion by 2032, growing at 8.49% CAGR. Valued at USD 13.38 billion in 2018.
data.description: On January 13, 2026, it was reported that the gas turbine market has shown robust and steady growth in recent years, underscoring its essential role in contemporary power generation and industrial uses. The market, which was worth approximately USD 13,379.41 million in 2018, has continued to expand significantly since then.
